svg.text: Phase 1 — Memory & data representation (before opening PMPP)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain CSAPP Chapter 2: how does integer overflow and floating point affect CUDA kernels?')
g.text: Integer overflow, float precision, bit tricks

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why is memory hierarchy understanding critical for CUDA programming?')
g.text: Cache lines, locality, bandwidth — the core of GPU perf

svg.text: Phase 2 — Execution model (parallel with PMPP Ch. 1–5)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does CSAPP Chapter 4 instruction-level knowledge help with CUDA optimization?')
sendPrompt('Explain ILP and loop unrolling from CSAPP Ch 5 and why it matters for GPU kernels')
sendPrompt('How does CSAPP Chapter 12 on concurrency map to CUDA thread management?')
sendPrompt('What from CSAPP Ch 9 virtual memory is needed for CUDA unified memory and pinned memory?')
g.text: Address spaces, page faults, memory mapping

svg.text: Skim only — useful context, not blocking

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the minimum I need from CSAPP Ch 3 assembly for GPU work?')
g.text: Read register model, skip x86 detail

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What from CSAPP Ch 8 exceptional control flow matters for GPU programming?')
sendPrompt('What from CSAPP Ch 7 linker is needed for CUDA development?')

svg.text: Skip — not relevant to your goals

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why can I skip CSAPP chapters on networking and I/O for GPU computing?')

svg.text: How PMPP 4th ed. maps to CSAPP prerequisites

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What CSAPP concepts should I have solid before reading PMPP Ch 1-3 on CUDA execution model?')
sendPrompt('Explain PMPP memory coalescing and tiling — what CSAPP memory concepts underpin this?')
sendPrompt('What CSAPP knowledge do I need for PMPP parallel patterns and reductions?')
g.text: Needs: Ch. 5, 12 — ILP and sync models

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should I understand from CSAPP before reading PMPP chapters on cuBLAS, cuDNN performance tuning?')

svg.text: On practice problems: do end-of-section practice problems only for Ch. 2, 5, 6.
Skip all homework problems. They're for CS undergrads, not for your use case.
